stem: According to a set of standards, a certain type of substance can contain a maximum of 0.002% sulfur by mass. If a sample of this substance has a mass of 230 grams, what is the maximum mass, in grams, of sulfur the sample can contain to meet these standards?
rationale: The correct answer is 0.0046 (or 23/5000). The maximum mass of sulfur is 0.002% of 230 grams, which is (0.002/100) × 230 = 0.00002 × 230 = 0.0046 grams.
